purpose: Common five-variant set for comparing Gmsh and snappyHexMesh full-aircraft mesh readiness.

variants.label: Span 720 reference
variants.description: Known wing-span +20 no-inlet reference case used in current Gmsh and snappyHexMesh evidence.
variants.reason: Anchor case with existing evidence from both meshing paths.
expected_mesh_stress: known small raw export boundary loop

variants.label: High aspect low sweep
variants.description: Longer span, smaller chords, and low sweep to stress thin wing/tip resolution.
variants.reason: Tests slender lifting-surface capture and tip/edge refinement.

variants.label: Low aspect high sweep
variants.description: Shorter span, large root chord, and high sweep to stress root blend and swept leading edge.
variants.reason: Tests swept-edge geometry capture and root/fuselage interaction.

variants.label: High taper aggressive tip
variants.description: Moderate span with large root chord and small tip chord to stress taper and tip closure.
variants.reason: Tests tip sharpness, taper-driven triangle quality, and local feature refinement.

variants.label: Broad chord negative sweep
variants.description: Broad chord planform with slight forward sweep to test less-common planform geometry within the current schema.
variants.reason: Tests mesher robustness away from the normal swept-wing family while staying inside continuous-variable bounds.
